title: Man charged with sexual assault and child sexual abuse material offences: R25-40267/49511/10733
content:
A
44-year-old
man,
Ryan James KNIGHT
, has been charged following two separate sexual assault investigations involving youths.
In March 2025, the Winnipeg Police Service became involved in an exploitation investigation regarding KNIGHT. Police learned that KNIGHT had been hired by a family to do renovation work. Over the period of eleven months, he formed a trusting relationship with the family, including spending time with their teenage son. Between August 2024 and March 2025, the teenager was groomed and spent independent time at KNIGHT’s residence. KNIGHT provided him a cell phone so he could contact the teenager. During this time, the teenager was sexually assaulted at KNIGHT’s residence. The abuse was not reported until later in the investigation.
Upon learning of KNIGHT’s past, the family ceased all ties with him, and he fled Winnipeg. KNIGHT repeatedly tried to contact them and they reported the harassment to police. As a result, a warrant for criminal harassment was issued.
On March 22, 2025, with the assistance of the Calgary Police Service, KNIGHT was arrested in Calgary, and several electronic devices were seized. KNIGHT and the seized items were returned to Winnipeg.
He was released on a release order.
A subsequent forensic examination of the devices revealed child sexual abuse material.
On July 27, 2025, KNIGHT was arrested near Mountain Avenue and Aikins Street. He was charged with the following offences:
Aggravated Sexual Assault
Sexual Interference
Makes, Prints, Publishes or Possess for the Purpose of Publication any Child Pornography
Possession of Child Pornography
He was detained in custody.
As a result of this arrest, a search warrant was executed at a residence in the 700 block of Atlantic Avenue, where data storage devices were seized.
The investigation continued and a second teenage survivor was identified. Police determined, KNIGHT was a family acquaintance and built a trusting relationship with the survivor and his parent.
On August 24,
2024
, the survivor was at KNIGHT’s residence for a sleepover when he was sexually assaulted. The abuse was not initially reported.
In October 2025, a forensic examination of all the seized devices revealed additional child sexual abuse material.
In consultation with the Crown, some of the initial charges that were laid in relation to the first survivor (in July), were stayed, but then re-laid along with the new charges pertaining to the second survivor (all charges shown below).
On January 25, 2026, the WPS Child Abuse Unit attended to the Headingley Correctional Centre and KNIGHT was arrested on the strength of an arrest warrant for the following charges:
Sexual Assault x 2
Sexual Interference x 2
Makes, Prints, Publishes or Possess for the Purpose of Publication any Child Pornography
Possession of Child Pornography x 2
Access any Child Pornography
Criminal Harassment
He remains in custody.
This investigation is continuing, anyone with information or who wishes to speak to an investigator can call the Child Abuse Unit at 204-986-3296.
The Winnipeg Police Service is committed to providing support to survivors of sexual assault. Resources include WPS Victim Services at 204-986-6350 and the Klinic Sexual Assault Crisis Line at 204-786-8631.

title: Statement from Chief Gene Bowers
content:
Earlier today, Elston Bostock was sentenced to seven years in prison for offences he pleaded guilty to. He has accepted responsibility for his actions and for the impact on public trust in the Winnipeg Police Service. His actions have no place within our ranks.
I understand this has shaken public confidence; the Service doesn’t take this knowledge lightly. At a media conference last August, I outlined the steps that have been initiated to restore accountability within the ranks of the Service and rebuild public confidence. In March, I will provide an update of these actions at a public meeting of the Winnipeg Police Board. I will be prepared to answer questions about our ongoing efforts when that meeting has concluded.
I want residents of Winnipeg to know that the overwhelming majority of members of the WPS serve with pride and professionalism, with the understanding that the actions of Elston Bostock are unacceptable. This case is a stain on our Service’s reputation; one that will serve as a reminder of our responsibility to uphold our values of integrity and accountability.

title: Suspect in custody following stabbing in Sargent Park area: C26-16788
content:
On January 21, 2026, The Winnipeg Police Service responded to the report of a stabbing in the 1000 block of Empress Street involving several individuals known to each other. Two men sustained injuries and were transported to hospital. The suspect was later located and taken into custody.
At approximately 6:10 p.m., a 27-year-old suspect entered a department store in the 1000 block of Empress Street, selected several items of clothing, and concealed the items under his jacket.
As the suspect exited the store, he encountered two men, aged 34 and 41, previously known to him, leading to a verbal dispute (not related to the theft of clothing). The suspect located a nearby fire extinguisher and sprayed the two men before emptying the remainder of the extinguisher about the store. The altercation moved into the parking lot, where it escalated to point where a knife was produced. Both men sustained injuries consistent with having been assaulted with a knife.
The suspect fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival. West District General Patrol officers attended and provided first aid to the victims until the Winnipeg Fire Paramedic Service arrived. Both were transported to hospital and later listed in stable condition.
At 11:13 p.m., North District General Patrol officers observed the suspect vehicle in the area of Alfred Avenue and Salter Street and conducted a traffic stop. A 27-year-old man was taken into custody, and a knife was seized during his arrest.
The investigation has been assumed by the Major Crimes Unit and remains ongoing.

had been charged with the following:
Aggravated Assault
Assault with a Weapon x 2
Mischief Over $5,000
Theft Under $5000

title: #DontDriveHigh: Drug Impaired Driving Enforcement Project Underway
content:
The Winnipeg Police Service, in partnership with Manitoba Public Insurance (MPI), is launching the Drug Impaired Driving Enforcement Project which will run until March 1. The enforcement initiative will see an increased number of officers focusing on drivers who may be under the influence of cannabis or other drugs.
While consuming cannabis isn't new, many are surprised by its varying effects on the body. Ingested cannabis can take up to four hours to reach its full effect and may impair drivers for as long as 12 hours, while inhaled cannabis can peak within 30 minutes. The message is clear: don’t drive high, including after consuming edibles.
Cannabis remains the second
‑
leading cause of impaired driving offences. This behaviour has the potential to lead to tragic outcomes, compromising the safety of all users of our roadways. During last year’s enforcement project, which ran in early 2025, the following results were captured:
302 traffic stops
97 individuals tested positive for recent cannabis consumption
86 tickets were issued, including for unlawful storage of cannabis in a vehicle and consuming cannabis in a vehicle (passenger).
This initiative supports the WPS Traffic Division’s ongoing commitment to road safety through both enforcement and education. Impaired driving remains a year
‑
round concern, and all motorists share responsibility for keeping roads safe.
An awareness campaign will run congruently with the enforcement initiative, reinforcing one key message to motorists:
#DontDriveHigh
. The campaign is funded in part by Manitoba Liquor & Lotteries.

title: Holiday Checkstop Program – Final Results
content:
Results:
December 21 to December 31, 2025 (New Year’s Eve)
442 vehicles stopped
29 drivers were found to be under the influence of alcohol
11 drivers who registered a WARN
6 immediate Roadside Prohibitions (ASD FAIL)
11 criminal charge for impaired/ 80mg% or over
1 refusal to comply with demand
During the 2025 Holiday Checkstop Program, the Winnipeg Police Service checked 2785 vehicles and identified 102 drivers operating under the influence of alcohol or cannabis.
15 people were charged with Criminal Impaired Driving (alcohol related)
77  people were issued Immediate Roadside Prohibitions
9 people failed drug screening tests (positive for cannabis)
146 Traffic Offence Tickets were issued
The drivers ages ranged from 22-53. The highest BAC was 280mg% - more than 3 times the statutory limit. Additional incidents included an impaired driver who was involved in a multi-vehicle collision; there were no injuries.
The 102 impaired-driving incidents that were detected during this year’s Checkstop Program serves as a reminder that public safety was unnecessarily put at risk 102 times.
The holidays may be over, but the responsibility to drive sober and keep our roads safe all year long is imperative. If you drink or use drugs, DO NOT DRIVE.
The 2025 Holiday Checkstop Program was supported through our partnership with Manitoba Liquor and Lotteries.

title: Man arrested in Crescentwood break and enter: C26-7559
content:
On January 9, 2026, around 11:30 p.m., officers from West District responded to the report of a residential break-in in the Crescentwood neighbourhood. The suspect had quickly fled the home after startling the residents.
Police met with the homeowners who reported being awaken by their security alarm and observed a man standing in their house. One of the residents yelled at him and he left the house. No thefts or damages were reported.
The man was located nearby and taken into custody. During the investigation, he was not forthcoming about his real identity and provided police with a false name.
Oliver Didtger EDERHOF (34)
, was charged with the following offences:
Break & Enter with Intent - Dwelling House
Public Mischief
Fail to Comply with Condition of Undertaking
He was detained in custody.

title: An overseas investigation leads to the sextortion arrest of Winnipeg teen: R25-56262
content:
On January 8, 2026, following an investigation by the Internet Child Exploitation (ICE) Unit, a teen
was arrested in regards to child sexual abuse imagery that was uploaded to a social media platform involving a youth female who resided in Belgium.
The accused from Winnipeg, formed an online contact with a teenage female from Belgium. They were not previously known to each other, nor did they ever meet in person. During the online contact which lasted approximately one year, the female was coerced to send intimate images of herself to the accused. He posted the images online without her consent.
Between October 4, 2025, and November 30, 2025, the female was harassed and extorted by the accused who demanded money in order to stop posting the intimate images online. The female reported the sextortion to police in Belgium and was assisted in having the images removed.
In October, 2025, the Winnipeg Police Service’s ICE Unit received an online report from the Canadian Centre for Child Protection (C3P) regarding a sextortion report that began in Belgium. Investigators identified a suspect address in Winnipeg.
A  search warrant was obtained for a residence in the Point Douglas neighbourhood of the city.
On January 8, 2026, the warrant was executed and a
15-year-old male
was taken into police custody. With the assistance of the Winnipeg Police Service’s Electronic Storage Device (ESD) dog, digital storage devices were located and seized.
The male was charged with the following offences:
Extortion
Criminal Harassment
Makes, Prints, Publishes or Poss for the Purpose of Publication any Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material
Make, Print, Publish, Distribute, Circulate or have in Possession for this Purpose Obscene Materials
Access any Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material
He was released by way of Release Order with numerous conditions.
The Winnipeg Police Service is committed to providing support to survivors of sexual exploitation. Anyone wishing to speak to an investigator can call the ICE Unit at 204-986-6172
or report crimes involving sextortion, intimate images and luring at
Cybertip
.
Resources include
WPS Victim Services
at 204-986-6350 and the Klinic Sexual Assault Crisis Line at 204-786-8631.

title: Man in custody regarding River-Osborne neighbourhood apartment fire: C26-8761
content:
On January 11, 2026, the Winnipeg Police Service arrested
52-year-old James Douglas LYSAK
, after responding to a residential fire at an apartment building in the 100 block of Clarke Street.
Shortly after 3:30 p.m., West District General Patrol officers attended to the above-mentioned apartment building and were advised by members of the Winnipeg Fire Paramedic Service (WFPS), that the fire was being investigated as suspicious in nature.
Through their investigation, officers learned that the suspect had set fire to furniture inside his apartment, which quickly spread, caused significant damage and endangered the lives of other tenants.

title: Three women randomly attacked in skywalk, man arrested: C26-5941
content:
On January 8. 2026, between 6:00 and 6:45 a.m., three women were randomly assaulted while walking to work through the Winnipeg Skywalk system.
At 6:30 a.m., a woman was walking through the Millennium Library Skywalk when a man grabbed hold of her and attempted to remove her backpack. She yelled at him to get away and fled to safety. She received a minor injury, but did not require medical attention.
Between 6:30 and 6:45 a.m., a second woman was walking along the Graham Avenue Skywalk when a man confronted her yelling obscenities and she was pushed against a railing. The suspect fled.
Shortly after, a third woman also walking along the Graham Avenue Skywalk was confronted by a man who threatened to stab her and slapped a coffee cup out of her hand. He picked up the cup, threw it at the woman and assaulted her by pushing her. The woman defended herself and he fled.
Police were notified about the assaults. Foot Patrol members quickly identified and located the suspect around 7:30 a.m. in the Skywalk near St. Mary Avenue.
Randy HERMAN (40)
, was charged with the following offences and detained in custody:
Robbery
Assault x 2
Assault with a Weapon
Uttering Threats - Cause Death or Bodily Harm
Members of the public are encouraged to report all criminal matters to the Winnipeg Police Service non-emergency 204-986-6222 or 911 for emergencies.

title: Community Notification
content:
The Manitoba Integrated High Risk Sex Offender Unit (MIHRSOU) is a joint forces unit of the Winnipeg Police Service and the RCMP. MIHRSOU today provides information regarding James Sheldon JASPER, 49 years of age, a convicted sex offender who is considered high risk to sexually re-offend against all children, particularly male children.
JASPER will be released from Headingley Correctional Centre on January 10, 2026, after serving a sentence for failing to comply with a condition on his Probation Order. JASPER was convicted on December 12, 2024, and received a sentence of 30 months less time in custody and a two-year Probation Order.
JASPER has been convicted previously of numerous sexual offences against children.  Although he has participated in sexual offender treatment in the past, JASPER is still considered high risk to re-offend. All children, particularly male children, are at risk of sexual violence.
Name: James Sheldon JASPER
DOB:  1976-08-03
Height:5’ 8” (173 cm.)
Weight: 210 lbs (95 kg)
Hair: Grey
Eyes:  Brown
Race:  Indigenous
Distinguishing marks: Scar on neck and forehead
If you have any information regarding JASPER, please contact the Manitoba Integrated High Risk Sex Offender Unit at (431) 489-8056. You may also contact your local RCMP Detachment or Manitoba Crime Stoppers at (204) 786-8477 or toll free at 1-800-222-8477.
Background Information:
James Sheldon JASPER is a 49-year-old male with a criminal record for sexual offences against children, particularly male children.
On November 25, 2003, JASPER was convicted of a sexual offence against a 10-year-old boy. JASPER invited the boy into his car, drove him to a secluded spot and sexually assaulted him. JASPER was sentenced to 26 months in jail followed by a 10-year Long-Term Supervision Order.  After completing the prison portion of his sentence, he was released on January 23, 2006. However, JASPER was subsequently convicted of breaching the terms of his Long-Term Supervision Order, in 2011 and 2016, and breaching the terms of his Probation Order in 2018 and 2021.
On April 3, 2020, JASPER was released after serving a 24-month sentence (6 months pre-sentence custody credit) at Stony Mountain Institution, regarding the 2018 convictions for breach of his Probation Order, for attending near a daycare, and being in possession of an electronic device.
JASPER was rearrested in August 2020, and convicted on June 17, 2021, for breach of his Probation Order by being in possession of an electronic device capable of accessing the internet, and received a sentence of 138 days (in addition to 462 days pre-sentence custody credit).
On March 7, 2023, JASPER was convicted of breaching his Probation Order for attending a park on August 21, 2022. Upon arrest, JASPER was in possession of colouring books, pencil crayons, a knife, binoculars and condoms. He received a sentence of 432 days (in addition to pre-sentence custody credit of 297 days) and 2 years supervised probation.
On December 12, 2024, JASPER was convicted of breaching his Probation Order for possessing a cell phone and was arrested on May 13, 2024. Prior to JASPER’s arrest the cellphone was confiscated by the group home manager. JASPER received a sentence of 30 months (pre-sentence custody credit of 321 days) and 2 years supervised probation.
Upon release JASPER will be subject to supervised probation, Weapons Prohibition Orders, and lifetime prohibition order. JASPER will be required to abide by court ordered conditions, including but not limited to:
Prohibited from attending a public park or public swimming area where persons under the age of 18 years are present or can reasonably be expected to be present, or a daycare centre, school ground, playground or community centre.
Prohibited from seeking, obtaining or continuing any employment, whether the employment is remunerated or not, or becoming or being a volunteer in a capacity, that involves being in a position of trust or authority towards persons under the age of 18 years.
Prohibited from having any contact with a person who is under the age of 18 years, unless he does so under the supervision of a person whom the court considers appropriate.
Prohibited from possessing weapons.
Prohibited from possessing a cellular phone.
Prohibited from photographing any child or view, access, possess or distribute any material or images of children of any kind who are or appear to be under the age of 18.
Prohibited from accessing, viewing, possessing or distributing any form of pornographic material of any kind accessed in any manner, including but not limited to imagery, stories, and written discussions/chats.
Prohibited from owning, possessing, use or acquire any personal computer system or electronic device capable of accessing the internet.
Observe a curfew of 11:00 pm – 7:00 am except for verifiable medical emergencies involving you or an immediate member of your family.
Notify Probation Officer of any person with whom you have any type of relationship.
Although JASPER has participated in sex offender treatment programs in the past, he is still considered high risk to re-offend.  Children - young boys in particular - are at risk of sexual violence. Some of JASPER’s victims have been “groomed” by him involving predatory, premeditated behaviour to access vulnerable victims, particularly young boys, to facilitate the sexual offences.
JASPER is expected to take up residence in Winnipeg.
This information is provided to enable members of the public to take suitable measures to protect themselves.  Any form of vigilante activity or other unreasonable conduct directed at JASPER will not be tolerated.
If you have information about JASPER, please call the Manitoba Integrated High Risk Sex Offender Unit at (431) 489-8056. You may also contact the Winnipeg Police Service at (204) 986-6222, your local RCMP Detachment, or Manitoba Crime Stoppers at (204) 786-8477 or toll free at 1-800-222-8477.  For media inquiries, please contact the Winnipeg Police Service Public Information Officer at (204) 986-3061.

title: Stolen vehicle, foot pursuit and seizure of a prohibited firearm: C26-7542
content:
On January 9, 2025, around 10:40 p.m., officers from East District Community Support Unit were patrolling the Talbot neighbourhood, when a vehicle was observed speeding northbound Gateway Road.
Police maintained a safe follow distance as the vehicle proceeded to evade police. Additional units flooded the area and the vehicle stopped at Molson Street/Norwich Avenue. The driver jumped out of the vehicle and began to run. After a short foot chase, police took the suspect into custody.
The vehicle was confirmed to be stolen and a semi-automatic carbine rifle with a loaded magazine was located inside.
Kyle Andrew CATHRO (37)
, was charged with the following offences:
Possession of a Firearm Knowing its Possession is Unauthorized
Unauthorized Possession of a Firearm in Motor Vehicle
Possession of a Firearm Knowing Serial Number has been Tampered With
Careless Storage of Firearm, Ammunition, Prohibited Weapon or Device
Possession of Firearm, Restricted/Prohibited Weapon, Firearm Part or Ammunition Contrary to Prohibition Order
Possession of Property Obtained by Crime Under $5000
He was detained in custody.

title: Hate-related graffiti investigation and arrest update: C26-1086
content:

Update
:
Investigators from the Major Crimes Division – Hate Crimes Section determined that on January 2, 2026, in the early morning hours, a man attended the Crescentwood area and spray painted random and hate-related graffiti on residential properties including garages, houses and vehicles.

title: Public Assistance sought in Norwood West restaurant arson investigation: C25-279897
content:
On November 20, 2025, at 9:30 p.m., the Winnipeg Police Service responded to a suspicious fire inside a restaurant in the 1-99 block St. Mary’s Road between Marion and Horace Streets.
Members of the Major Crimes Unit patrolling the area were dispatched and attended the restaurant shortly after 10:00 p.m. During their investigation, it was learned that a group of five adults (two women, three men), attended the restaurant where they ordered food and alcoholic beverages.
When it came time to settle the bill for the consumed items, a fire was set inside the restaurant causing significant damage. The fire was extinguished prior to the arrival of emergency personnel and no injuries were reported. All suspects fled on foot before police arrived.
Due to the nature of the incident, investigators believe the fire was deliberately set as a diversion to facilitate the suspects’ escape.
Fire Investigators of the Major Crimes Unit have assumed the investigation and are requesting public assistance in locating the following female suspect:
She is described as follows:
Indigenous/Metis
20-30 years
Medium build
Brown/red curly hair
Black tank top
Tattoo on chest/breast area
CCTV suspect image
:
Image
Anyone with information regarding this suspect is asked to contact the Winnipeg Police Service’s non-emergency line at
204-986-6222 or anonymously at Crime Stoppers 204-786-TIPS or
winnipegcrimestoppers.org
.

title: North End traffic stop leads to seizure of two firearms – three in custody: C26-3249
content:
On January 5, 2026, North District General Patrol and Community Support Unit officers arrested three individuals following a traffic stop involving a 2020 Ford Explorer in the 200 block of Machray Avenue. Two firearms were located in the vehicle and seized as evidence.
Shortly after midnight, officers observed the Ford Explorer commit a Highway Traffic Act infraction at Charles Street and Machray Avenue which resulted in a traffic stop in the rear lane at the above-mentioned address. As the vehicle came to a stop, the driver and two passengers fled on foot. Officers arrested a 46-year-old passenger outside the vehicle. He was found with almost $3,000 and 17 grams of Fentanyl on his person.
A second male passenger, also aged 46 years, and the 37-year-old woman driver were located at the intersection of Main Street and Cathedral Avenue where they were taken into custody without incident.
Officers searched the vehicle and seized the following:
A dismantled Remington 812 12-guage shotgun
A loaded Dan Wesson .357 revolver with a defaced serial number
A digital scale
Chantel Kim TRAVERSE (37)
is charged with multiple firearms-related offences and Fail to Comply with Condition of Undertaking x 2.
Ronald Jason LEATHWOOD (46)
is charged with firearms-related offences, drug trafficking, possession of proceeds of crime, and Fail to Comply with Release Order x 5.
Robert Allan THOMPSON (46)
is charged with similar firearms-related offences.
TRAVERSE, LEATHWOOD, and THOMPSON were detained in police custody awaiting further disposition.

title: Parking lot collision and assault leads to arrest and multiple charges: C26-2962
content:
On the afternoon of January 4, 2026, the Winnipeg Police Service responded to a disturbance in the 1400 block of Ellice Avenue stemming from motor vehicle collision in a parking lot. A 38-year-old man was arrested by responding West District officers and detained in custody.
At approximately 4:45 p.m., a 2013 Dodge Dart operated by the 38-year-old suspect, was observed driving erratically in the area of King Edward Street and Ness Avenue. Moments later, he was involved in a motor vehicle collision in a parking lot in the 1400 block of Ellice Avenue where he threatened the 52-year-old driver of the other involved vehicle. The incident was witnessed by bystanders who contacted police.
When a responding Community Support Unit (CSU) officer arrived, he observed the suspect assaulting a 23-year-old security guard who had intervened in the altercation. With assistance from responding West District General Patrol units and the security guard, the combative male was safely taken into custody. The CSU officer was physically assaulted during the arrest and sustained minor injuries which did not require medical attention. The security guard was uninjured.
Bobby Roger COUTU (38)
is charged with the following offences:
Assault a Peace Officer
Resist Peace Officer
Assault
Uttering Threats – Cause Death or Bodily Harm
Causing a Disturbance
Operation of a Conveyance While Impaired to Any Degree by BAC or Drug or Combination of Both
Fail or Refuse to Comply with Demand
Fail to Comply with Condition of Release Order
In addition, COUTU was issued a number of Highway Traffic Act Offence Notices including driving without a license and driving an unregistered vehicle. He was also arrested on the strength on two separate warrants of arrest for Fail to Attend Court and Fail to Comply with Release Order.
COUTU was detained in custody.

title: Officers intervene after observing unprovoked downtown assault – man in custody: C26-2812
content:
On January 4, 2026, patrolling Winnipeg Police Service officers arrested a 30-year-old man involved in the random, unprovoked assault of a woman in her 30’s in the 300 block of Portage Avenue.
At 1:30 p.m., West District General Patrol officers were travelling westbound on Portage Avenue near Donald Street when they observed a man physically assaulting a woman and knocking her to the ground. The officers quickly intervened, stopped the assault, and arrested the uncooperative suspect. During the arrest, one officer deployed a conductive energy weapon to safely take the suspect into custody. The woman suffered minor injuries and did not require medical attention.
The suspect and the victim were not previously known to one another and the assault appears to be random and unprovoked.
Jacob GEORGE (30)
has been charged with Assault and was detained in police custody awaiting further disposition.

title: Update: Investigators identify and arrest suspect in pellet gun shooting of transit operator: C25-256607
content:
On October 27, 2025, a Winnipeg Transit operator was assaulted shot in the hand with what is believed to be a pellet gun during a dispute with a fare. The incident was assigned to the Major Crimes Unit and the investigation continued. As a result of the investigation, a suspect was identified.
On January 4, 2026, a
14-year-old boy
was arrested at his residence in East Kildonan, and is facing the following charges:
Assault with a Weapon
Fail to Comply with Condition of Undertaking x 2
Mischief Under $5,000/Obstruct in Enjoyment
He was detained in custody.
As previously released:
On October 27, 2025 at 2:11 p.m., East District General Patrol officers responded to the intersection of Gateway Road and Springfield Road for the report of a youth that produced what is believed to be a pellet gun and shot a transit operator in the hand following a dispute. The operator received minor injuries and was transported to hospital in stable condition.
With the assistance of the Tactical Support Team and K9, officers searched the area for the youth, however, the youth had fled prior to police arrival.
The major Crimes Unit has assumed the investigation.

title: Shots fired in North End neighbourhood leads to multiple arrests - firearm, drugs seized: C26-1664
content:
On January 3, 2026, members of the Winnipeg Police Service responded to the 300 block of Manitoba Avenue for the report of shots fired outside of a residence. Officers arrested seven individuals into custody and seized a 9mm handgun, a quantity of illicit drugs, and $925 as evidence.
The investigation revealed that at approximately 2:40 a.m., a 38-year-old man and a 20-year-old woman became involved in a heated verbal argument inside a residence in the above-mentioned block. The argument escalated when the man physically assaulted the woman, causing minor injuries. Shortly after, while armed with a handgun, he exited the building, and recklessly discharged the firearm, striking a neighbouring residence. No one inside the neighbouring residence reported any injuries as a result of the firearm discharge.
Attending North District General Patrol officers, assisted by members of the K9 Unit and General Patrol officers from the Central and East Districts, located the 38-year-old man and the 20-year-old woman involved along with five additional people inside the residence where the assault had originated. All were arrested without incident.
During the subsequent execution of a search warrant for the residence, officers located and seized the following property:
A 9mm Roebuck handgun with a magazine containing 9mm ammunition
Approximately 3.6 Ounces of Fentanyl (Estimated Street Value: $5,400)
Approximately 3.9 Ounces of Methamphetamine (Estimated Street Value: $1,300)
Alexander Paul DOYLE (38)
has been charged with multiple firearms-related and drug trafficking-related offences, Assault, and Proceeds of Crime under $5,000. He was detained in custody.
Tyesha Sampaio LOBO (28)
, and
Travis Adam CHARTRAND (42)
were charged with similar firearms-related and drug-trafficking offences and were also detained in custody.
Two men in their 50’s
and
two women in their 20’s
face similar charges and were released on either Appearance Notices or Undertakings.

title: Man arrested in possession of loaded handgun and prohibited magazine following attempted assault on taxi driver: C26-2518
content:
On January 4, 2026, a 48-year-old man was detained following an attempted assault on a taxi driver in the William Whyte area. Officers subsequently arrested the man after locating a loaded handgun and a prohibited 13-round magazine on his person.
At approximately 1:40 a.m., an assault on a taxi driver was developing in the area of Burrows Avenue and Powers Street. The taxi driver observed a marked police cruiser and activated their emergency strobe light, grabbing the attention of the North District General Patrol officers and prompting the man to disengage.
Officers made a U-turn to investigate and observed the taxi pulled over in the 200 block of Powers Street and the suspect walking away. Officers spoke with the driver, who reported the man walking away had attempted to assault him. He was not injured as a result of the incident.
Officers detained the man in the 200 block of Powers Street, where he was found to be in possession of a loaded Springfield Hellcat 9x19 handgun with a prohibited 13 round magazine.
Robert Louis PARISIAN (48)
has been charged with:
Possession of Firearm, Restricted/Prohibited Weapon, Firearm Part or Ammunition Contrary to Prohibition Order
Possession of a Prohibited or Restricted Weapon Knowing its Possession is Unauthorized
Possession of a Prohibited or Restricted Firearm with Ammunition
Carrying Concealed Weapon Prohibited Device or Ammunition
PARISIAN was detained in custody.

title: Two arrested after convenience store clerk assaulted with a weapon: C26-2238
content:
On January 3, 2026 a man and a woman assaulted a convenience store employee with a weapon in the 400 block of Talbot Avenue. The clerk received minor injuries from the assault and did not require medical attention.
At approximately 7:12 p.m., a 37-year-old male and a 40-year-old female became involved in a dispute with a store clerk at the above-mentioned convenience store. During the dispute, the clerk was assaulted with various items obtained from within the store. The incident was interrupted by a person who entered the store during the assault, and police were contacted. The suspects left before police arrived.
East District General Patrol officers attended and reviewed security footage of the incident. While officers were speaking with the store clerk, the two suspects returned to the store, attempted to make a false claim that they had been assaulted by the employee, and were subsequently arrested.
Nico Randy SYLVESTRE (37)
has been charged with Assault with a Weapon. He was detained in custody.
A 40-year-old woman
is facing a charge of Assault with a Weapon. She was released on an Undertaking.

title: Man arrested after a fast food robbery: C26-488
content:
On January 1, 2026, around 2:45 p.m., officers from West District with the assistance of the Tactical Support Team, responded to a suspect boarding a transit bus following a commercial robbery reported moments earlier.
A man entered a fast-food restaurant in the 1500 block of Portage Avenue and demanded  money from the front till and threatened to shoot the employees.
The three employees ran to safety and the suspect circled around the counter and unsuccessfully attempted to open the cash register. He then entered the kitchen, and stole hamburgers and fries that were still cooking in frying pans. He exited the restaurant and boarded a transit bus where he proceeded to eat the food and tried to fight people on the bus. Nobody reported any assaults.
The bus had not travelled far, when it stopped and the suspect was taken into police custody. He was in possession of a stolen wallet.
Jonathan FONTAINE (29)
, was charged with the following offences:
Robbery
Possession of Property Obtained by Crime Under $5000
He was detained in custody.

title: Officers threatened and assaulted during shoplifting arrest: C25-313840
content:
On December 30, 2025, around 6:30 p.m., members of the Foot Patrol Unit were providing community engagement in the Central District. While at a store in the 300 block of Portage Avenue, they were alerted to someone attempting to leave the store without paying for merchandise.
The suspect was placed under arrest and he verbally threatened to harm one of the officers. An additional unit attended for transport purposes.
As the man was being placed into the police cruiser car he began to fight and one of the officers was kicked in the head. The officer did not require medical attention at the time.
A
37-year-old man
is facing the following charges:
Assault Peace Officer
Uttering Threats - Cause Death or Bodily Harm
Theft Under $5000
He was detained in custody.
